findings.title: No threat model file found in .mcp/, docs/, or security/
evidence: Searched for: threat-model.json, threat-model.md under .mcp/, docs/, security/
requiredActions: Create a threat model document (threat-model.json or threat-model.md) in .mcp/, docs/, or security/.
Include STRIDE analysis, OWASP Top-10 mapping, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, trust boundaries, and data flow diagrams.
Reference the threat model from your PR description and link it to changed components.

findings.title: No SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) found in repository
evidence: Searched for CycloneDX (.cdx.json, bom.json, bom.xml) and SPDX (.spdx, .spdx.json) files.
requiredActions: Generate an SBOM in CycloneDX or SPDX format and commit it to the repository.
For Node.js: `npx @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm --output-file sbom.json`.
For Python: `cyclonedx-bom -o sbom.json`.
Automate SBOM generation in CI so it stays current with every dependency change.
Ensure the SBOM is included in any artifact upload to your registry (SLSA Level 2+).

findings.title: Secret detected in git history (3 findings)
requiredActions: Secrets in git history remain exposed even after removal from the working tree.
Rotate all exposed credentials immediately.
Use git-filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner to purge the secrets from history, then force-push and notify all collaborators to re-clone.
Enable branch protection and secret scanning alerts on the remote host.

findings.title: No secret manager usage detected — secrets may be hardcoded or in env files
requiredActions: Integrate a cloud secret manager appropriate for your platform:
• AWS: AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store (SecureString)
• GCP: Secret Manager with Workload Identity
• Azure: Azure Key Vault with Managed Identity
• Multi-cloud / self-hosted: HashiCorp Vault, Doppler, or 1Password Secrets Automation
Never store secrets in environment files committed to the repo, CI log output, or container images.

findings.title: Lambda function URL with no authentication — publicly invocable by anyone
requiredActions: Set authorization_type = "AWS_IAM" on all aws_lambda_function_url resources.
Use a CloudFront distribution with signed URLs or an API Gateway with IAM/Cognito auth as an alternative.
If public invocation is intentional, add CORS restrictions and rate limiting.

findings.title: No aws_flow_log resource found — VPC network traffic is unlogged, lateral movement and exfiltration undetectable
requiredActions: Add an aws_flow_log resource for each VPC and ship logs to CloudWatch Logs or S3.
Set flow log aggregation interval to 1 minute for near-real-time detection.
Create CloudWatch metric filters and alarms for rejected traffic spikes.

findings.title: No VPC endpoint found for AWS services — traffic routes over public internet
requiredActions: Create aws_vpc_endpoint resources for S3, ECR (api + dkr), and other frequently used AWS services.
Use Gateway endpoints for S3/DynamoDB (free) and Interface endpoints for other services.
Set the VPC endpoint policy to restrict access to specific S3 buckets or ECR repositories.

findings.title: No GuardDuty detector resource found — threat detection (credential misuse, crypto-mining) disabled
requiredActions: Add an aws_guardduty_detector resource with enable = true to your Terraform.
Enable GuardDuty in all AWS regions and aggregate findings into a delegated admin account.
Subscribe to GuardDuty findings via EventBridge and route high-severity alerts to your on-call channel.

findings.title: AWS Security Hub not enabled — findings from GuardDuty/Inspector/Macie not centrally aggregated
requiredActions: Add an aws_securityhub_account resource to your Terraform.
Enable the AWS Foundational Security Best Practices and CIS AWS Foundations standards.
Aggregate Security Hub findings across regions and accounts into a central delegated admin.

findings.title: No SBOM found. Syft is available — run it to generate one.
requiredActions: Run: syft . -o cyclonedx-json=.mcp/sbom/latest.json
Or set SECURITY_AUTO_SBOM=true to auto-generate on each gate run.

findings.title: No SLSA provenance attestation found
requiredActions: Generate SLSA provenance during CI/CD build.
Use slsa-github-generator or equivalent to produce .intoto.jsonl attestations.
Store provenance in .mcp/attestations/

findings.title: IR playbook missing: Cloud misconfiguration (security/playbooks/cloud-misconfiguration.md)
requiredActions: Create the IR playbook at security/playbooks/cloud-misconfiguration.md.
Include all required sections: detection criteria, escalation path, containment, eradication, recovery, communication, post-incident review, and MTTD/MTTR targets.

findings.title: IR playbook missing: Ransomware (security/playbooks/ransomware.md)
requiredActions: Create the IR playbook at security/playbooks/ransomware.md.
Include all required sections: detection criteria, escalation path, containment, eradication, recovery, communication, post-incident review, and MTTD/MTTR targets.

findings.title: Destructive or validation-skipping toggle enabled (force_destroy / skip_final_snapshot / disable_rollback)
requiredActions: Set skip_final_snapshot = false on RDS so a snapshot is taken before deletion.
Remove force_destroy = true from buckets holding real data; require manual emptying instead.
Add a lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } block to critical stateful resources.
Keep skip_provider_registration / disable_rollback at their safe defaults.

findings.title: IaC creates a publicly exposed resource (public-read ACL or publicly_accessible database)
requiredActions: Remove acl = "public-read"/"public-read-write"; use bucket policies with explicit principals instead.
Set publicly_accessible = false on all database instances and place them in private subnets.
Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account and bucket level to override accidental public ACLs.
Front any legitimately public asset bucket with CloudFront + Origin Access Control, not a public ACL.

findings.title: aws_s3_bucket has no server-side encryption and/or no public access block resource — data may be unencrypted or publicly exposable
requiredActions: Add a server-side encryption configuration:
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "this" {
bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
rule { apply_server_side_encryption_by_default { sse_algorithm = "aws:kms" } }
Add a public access block:
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "this" {
bucket                  = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
block_public_acls       = true
block_public_policy     = true
ignore_public_acls      = true
restrict_public_buckets = true
Verify: checkov -d . --check CKV2_AWS_6,CKV_AWS_19 ; trivy config .

findings.title: EC2 instance must enforce IMDSv2 with hop limit 1 — SSRF in the application reaches 169.254.169.254 and steals the instance-profile credentials when IMDSv1 is allowed.
requiredActions: Add metadata_options { http_tokens = "required", http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } to the aws_instance so IMDSv2 session tokens are mandatory and the metadata endpoint is unreachable from a single-hop SSRF.

findings.title: RDS instance must not be publicly accessible — A publicly accessible RDS instance is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and CVE exploitation.
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_db_instance and place it in private subnets behind a security group that only allows the application tier.

findings.title: RDS storage must be encrypted — Unencrypted RDS storage exposes the database contents if the underlying volume or a snapshot is accessed.
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_db_instance. Note: encryption can only be enabled at creation; rebuild from an encrypted snapshot if the instance already exists.

findings.title: S3 bucket must not use a public ACL — A public-read ACL lets anonymous users list and download every object in the bucket.
requiredActions: Set acl = "private" and attach an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with all four block flags = true.

findings.title: S3 bucket must have a public access block — Without a public access block, a future bucket policy or ACL can silently expose the bucket to the internet.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block referencing this bucket with block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, and restrict_public_buckets all set to true.

findings.title: KMS key must have automatic rotation enabled — A customer-managed KMS key without rotation means a single key compromise exposes all historical ciphertext.
requiredActions: Set enable_key_rotation = true on the aws_kms_key.

findings.title: Lambda function URL must require IAM auth — A Lambda function URL with authorization_type NONE is an unauthenticated internet endpoint that can reach the function's IAM role and downstream data stores.
requiredActions: Set authorization_type = "AWS_IAM" on the aws_lambda_function_url, or front it with API Gateway / a Lambda authorizer that enforces authentication.

findings.title: S3 bucket should enable versioning — Without versioning, an accidental or malicious overwrite/delete of objects is unrecoverable (no ransomware rollback).
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource for this bucket with versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" }.

findings.title: RDS instance must enable deletion protection — Without deletion protection, a compromised credential or accidental apply can destroy the production database.
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_db_instance.

findings.title: RDS instance must set a backup retention period — A zero/short backup retention window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive or ransomware event.
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 (days) on the aws_db_instance.

findings.title: S3 bucket should declare server-side encryption — A bucket without an explicit server-side encryption configuration relies on account defaults that may not use a CMK.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration for this bucket using aws:kms (or AES256).

findings.title: S3 bucket should enable access logging — Without access logging, bulk object reads (data exfiltration) leave no forensic trail.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_logging resource for this bucket. Choose a separate, access-restricted target_bucket for the logs.

controlCoverage.description: All services enforce authentication and authorization with no implicit trust.
Secrets stored only in a dedicated secret manager.
TLS configuration is verified.
CSP is configured without unsafe inline execution.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: CSRF protections and tests are present.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: SSRF guards and tests are present.
Mobile release configuration meets MASVS baseline.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: AI outputs are schema-validated and tools are allowlisted.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Access control policies enforce least privilege and separation of duties (NIST 800-53 AC).
Audit events are logged, immutable, timestamped, and retained ≥1 year (NIST 800-53 AU).
All users and services are uniquely identified and authenticated (NIST 800-53 IA).
All data in transit is encrypted using approved algorithms (NIST 800-53 SC).
All inputs are validated, sanitized, and encoded before processing (NIST 800-53 SI).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Incident response playbooks exist, are tested, and have defined MTTD/MTTR SLAs (NIST 800-53 IR).
Cardholder data at rest is encrypted and PAN is never stored in plaintext (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 3).
Cardholder data in transit uses TLS 1.2+ with approved cipher suites (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 4).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Logical access to systems is restricted and uses MFA for privileged accounts (SOC 2 CC6).
System components are monitored for anomalous activity and security events (SOC 2 CC7).
Controls in place to prevent initial access vectors: phishing, supply chain, public-facing exploits (TA0001).
Controls prevent credential dumping, brute force, and credential theft (TA0006).
Controls prevent unauthorized data exfiltration over network, cloud storage, or encoding (TA0010).
Logging is tamper-evident; code signing prevents unauthorized binary substitution (TA0005).
Network segmentation and mTLS prevent lateral movement between services (TA0008).
Data backups are encrypted and tested; ransomware-resistant backup policies in place (TA0040).
Egress filtering blocks C2 channels; DNS monitoring detects beaconing (TA0011).
LLM outputs are validated against a schema before use in downstream systems (OWASP LLM02).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Rate limiting and input length limits prevent model resource exhaustion (OWASP LLM04).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: LLM plugins and tools operate on an explicit allowlist with minimal permissions (OWASP LLM07).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: LLM agents cannot take irreversible actions without human confirmation (OWASP LLM08).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: ML decision systems affecting people carry fairness/bias evaluation (disparate impact, equalized odds, demographic parity) and representativeness evidence.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Secrets and PII are redacted/tokenized before reaching any LLM payload, preventing shadow-AI data leakage to model providers.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Build artifacts have signed SLSA Level 3 provenance from a hermetic, ephemeral CI build.
Network microsegmentation enforces deny-by-default between all services.
Every request is continuously verified — no persistent trust based on network location.
Business logic abuse scenarios (state skipping, negative values, race conditions) are tested before release.
Proxy and origin servers normalize CL/TE headers; H2C upgrade disabled.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: DNS audit confirms no dangling CNAME records pointing to unprovisioned services.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Multimodal inputs (image, audio, document) are treated as untrusted and cannot inject instructions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Agent memory and scratchpad content is validated before use in downstream instructions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Model cannot be probed to recite training PII or memorized secrets.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Mobile release binary uses code obfuscation and anti-instrumentation controls (MASVS-RESILIENCE).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: EMV 3DS version 2.2+ is used for all card-not-present high-risk transactions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Protected Health Information (PHI) is encrypted at rest and in transit; access is limited and audited.
A dedicated secrets scanner is installed and runnable.
Infrastructure scanners and policy-as-code tooling are installed and runnable.
A container scanner is installed and runnable.
An SBOM generator (Syft) is installed and runnable.
Baseline configurations are established and enforced for all systems (NIST 800-53 CM).
Third-party components are assessed for security risk before adoption (NIST 800-53 SA).
Supply chain integrity is verified via SBOM, provenance attestation, and dependency scanning (NIST 800-53 SR).
Network security controls restrict inbound/outbound traffic to only what is necessary (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 1).
All software components are up-to-date and protected against known vulnerabilities (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 6).
Controls detect and prevent backdoors, scheduled tasks, and unauthorized service installation (TA0003).
Containers and services cannot escalate to root; least-privilege IAM enforced (TA0004).
A CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM is generated on every release and stored as a signed artifact.
Incident response playbooks exist for all surfaces, are tested within 90 days, and have contact lists.
Container images comply with CIS Docker Benchmark Level 2.
Kubernetes cluster configuration complies with CIS Kubernetes Benchmark Level 2.
Cloud account configuration complies with CIS Benchmark Level 2 for the active cloud provider.
Kubernetes pods enforce security contexts with least-privilege settings.
Kubernetes NetworkPolicy resources restrict pod-to-pod traffic.
All database connections require TLS/SSL encryption in transit.
Database credentials use least-privilege accounts, not root/admin.
Only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms are used (AES-256, SHA-256+, RSA-2048+).
PII, PAN, SSN, and credentials are never written to application logs.
EC2 instances enforce IMDSv2 (HttpTokens=required) — IMDSv1 disabled.
AWS GuardDuty / GCP SCC / Azure Defender for Cloud is enabled across all accounts.
All CI/CD GitHub Actions are SHA-pinned; GITHUB_TOKEN has minimal permissions; no pwn-request risk.

confidence.summary: Automated coverage 2%, scanner readiness 0%, missing controls 48, risk-accepted controls 0. Change type: infra.

rule { apply_server_side_encryption_by_default { sse_algorithm = "aws:kms" } }
Add a public access block:
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "this" {
bucket                  = aws_s3_bucket.this.id
block_public_acls       = true
block_public_policy     = true
ignore_public_acls      = true
restrict_public_buckets = true
Verify: checkov -d . --check CKV2_AWS_6,CKV_AWS_19 ; trivy config .

newFindings.title: EC2 instance must enforce IMDSv2 with hop limit 1 — SSRF in the application reaches 169.254.169.254 and steals the instance-profile credentials when IMDSv1 is allowed.
requiredActions: Add metadata_options { http_tokens = "required", http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } to the aws_instance so IMDSv2 session tokens are mandatory and the metadata endpoint is unreachable from a single-hop SSRF.

newFindings.title: RDS instance must not be publicly accessible — A publicly accessible RDS instance is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and CVE exploitation.
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_db_instance and place it in private subnets behind a security group that only allows the application tier.

newFindings.title: RDS storage must be encrypted — Unencrypted RDS storage exposes the database contents if the underlying volume or a snapshot is accessed.
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_db_instance. Note: encryption can only be enabled at creation; rebuild from an encrypted snapshot if the instance already exists.

newFindings.title: S3 bucket must not use a public ACL — A public-read ACL lets anonymous users list and download every object in the bucket.
requiredActions: Set acl = "private" and attach an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with all four block flags = true.

newFindings.title: S3 bucket must have a public access block — Without a public access block, a future bucket policy or ACL can silently expose the bucket to the internet.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block referencing this bucket with block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, and restrict_public_buckets all set to true.

newFindings.title: KMS key must have automatic rotation enabled — A customer-managed KMS key without rotation means a single key compromise exposes all historical ciphertext.
requiredActions: Set enable_key_rotation = true on the aws_kms_key.

newFindings.title: Lambda function URL must require IAM auth — A Lambda function URL with authorization_type NONE is an unauthenticated internet endpoint that can reach the function's IAM role and downstream data stores.
requiredActions: Set authorization_type = "AWS_IAM" on the aws_lambda_function_url, or front it with API Gateway / a Lambda authorizer that enforces authentication.

newFindings.title: S3 bucket should enable versioning — Without versioning, an accidental or malicious overwrite/delete of objects is unrecoverable (no ransomware rollback).
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource for this bucket with versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" }.

newFindings.title: RDS instance must enable deletion protection — Without deletion protection, a compromised credential or accidental apply can destroy the production database.
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_db_instance.

newFindings.title: RDS instance must set a backup retention period — A zero/short backup retention window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive or ransomware event.
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 (days) on the aws_db_instance.

newFindings.title: S3 bucket should declare server-side encryption — A bucket without an explicit server-side encryption configuration relies on account defaults that may not use a CMK.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration for this bucket using aws:kms (or AES256).

newFindings.title: S3 bucket should enable access logging — Without access logging, bulk object reads (data exfiltration) leave no forensic trail.
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_logging resource for this bucket. Choose a separate, access-restricted target_bucket for the logs.

resolvedFindings.title: package.json has no dependencies/devDependencies
requiredActions: Verify this is intentional. If not, add dependencies with pinned ranges.

resolvedFindings.title: AI/tooling present but bounded output (schema validation) not detected
requiredActions: Enforce bounded outputs via JSON schema validation for every AI response used by code.
Add prompt-injection defenses: input sanitization, tool allowlists, deny-by-default tool router, and sensitive data redaction.

resolvedFindings.title: Potential prompt injection cues detected. Requires explicit mitigations and tests.
requiredActions: Add multi-layer prompt-injection protection: instruction hierarchy enforcement, content isolation, tool gating, and output validation.
Add a red-team test harness with injection payloads and exfil attempts.

resolvedFindings.title: AI tool definitions detected without apparent allowlist enforcement
requiredActions: Implement a tool allowlist: only expose tools the model is permitted to call.
Require human approval for high-impact tool calls (delete, execute, send).
Apply principle of least privilege to all agentic capabilities.

resolvedFindings.title: ML decision system detected with no fairness / bias-testing artifact
requiredActions: Add fairness evaluation (e.g. Fairlearn, AIF360, Aequitas) measuring disparate impact, equalized odds, and demographic parity across protected attributes for any model that affects people (EU AI Act high-risk obligations; NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.11 / MANAGE).
Document the training-data representativeness assessment and bias-mitigation steps as model-card evidence (ISO 42001).
Gate model promotion on fairness thresholds in CI and re-evaluate on every retrain to catch drift-induced bias.
